title: Adner
content:

{{Quote|Etsero runs the largest gambling house on the pleasure planet of Adner.|Airen Cracken, ''Wanted by Cracken''|<ref name="WbC" />}}
'''Adner''' was a [[Planet/Legends|planet]] located in the [[Expansion Region/Legends|Expansion Region's]] [[Yataga sector/Legends|Yataga sector]]. It had [[Binary star/Legends|two suns]] and was a [[Temperature/Legends|hot]] world with cold, [[Water/Legends|arid]] [[Standard day/Legends|nights]]. Adner was the [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of the [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Adnerem]] [[Species/Legends|species]]. The base unit of the Adnerem society was the [[steris]], a relatively small group of [[Gene/Legends|genetically]]-unbounded individuals.

Two [[Millennium/Legends|millennia]] before the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], the Adnerem transformed their homeworld into a famous pleasure planet, featuring popular [[Casino/Legends|casinos]], pleasure houses, and [[Amusement park|theme parks]]. In the entertainment venues on Adner, the natives chiefly employed members of other species.

The Adnerem [[Etsero]] ran the [[The Red Lisken|largest casino]] on Adner. By [[7 ABY/Legends|7 ABY]], Etsero was in debt to an unnamed party, and as a result, his casino was used to launder [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] for [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] interests in the [[Sector/Legends|sector]]. The [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] dispatched an [[Unidentified New Republic agent (The Red Liskin)|agent]] to put an end to the fraud and suspected [[Death/Legends|murders]] taking place in Etsero's casino.

==Description==
{{Quote|Make a mistake, and you'll never see another twin sunrise over Adner.|Airen Cracken, warning New Republic agents about Etsero in ''Wanted by Cracken''|<ref name="WbC" />}}
Adner was a terrestrial [[Planet/Legends|planet]]<ref name="Journal11" /> located in the [[Adner system/Legends|Adner system]]. It was a part of the [[Yataga sector/Legends|Yataga sector]],<ref name="EAOC" /> within the [[Trailing Sectors/Legends|Trailing Sectors]] portion of<ref name="Trailing Sectors" /> the [[Expansion Region/Legends|Expansion Region]].<ref name="EAOC" /> Adner [[Orbit/Legends|orbited]] a [[Binary star/Legends|pair]] of bright [[Color/Legends|reddish]] [[Star/Legends|stars]] that provided the planet with a [[Temperature/Legends|hot]] climate, although the [[Standard day/Legends|nights]] on Adner were cold and [[Water/Legends|arid]].<ref name="Journal11" />

At some point, Adner was [[Homeworld/Legends|home]] to a semi-social [[Adnerem precursor species|species]] of [[Diet/Legends|omnivorous]] [[Scavenger/Legends|scavenger]]-[[Hunting/Legends|hunters]]. The [[Species/Legends|species]] eventually evolved into the [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Adnerem]].<ref name="Journal11" />

==History==
{{Quote|In an attempt to stop these illegal activities, an agent was dispatched to Etsero's den, ''The Red Lisken''. He never reported in and it is believed he may have met with foul play at Etsero's hands.|Airen Cracken, ''Wanted by Cracken''|<ref name="WbC" />}}
[[File:Etsero.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Etsero owned the largest casino on Adner.]]
Adner was discovered by a [[Duros/Legends|Duros]] [[Scout/Legends|scout]] 5,000 [[Standard year/Legends|years]] before the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. Overall, the history of Adner consisted of either individual Adnerem social groups, called [[Steris|steri]], achieving or losing power, or the strategic movement of individual Adnerem between different steri. Such changes usually resulted in cold wars and [[raid-war]]s—quick and small-scale conflicts unique to the Adnerem.<ref name="Journal11" />

Two thousand years before the Galactic Civil War, the Adnerem transformed their homeworld into a<ref name="Journal11" /> famous<ref name="SoF">''[[Suns of Fortune]]''</ref> entertainment<ref name="Journal11" /> and pleasure world<ref name="SoF" /> in order to fund their strategy of planetary self-sufficiency.<ref name="Journal11" /> At some point before the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]]<ref name="ND" /> in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]],<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> the [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]] [[Captain/Legends|captain]] and [[Gambler/Legends|gambler]] [[Theol Dunoche]] visited [[Kala'uun/Legends|Kala'uun]],<ref name="ND" /> a [[Spaceport/Legends|spaceport]] on the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]] planet [[Ryloth/Legends|Ryloth]],<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> where he kidnapped two [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] [[Gender/Legends|women]] about to be sold into [[Slavery/Legends|slavery]]. Dunoche, who had a [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] posted on him as a result, befriended the Twi'leks, and the latter came to work as dealers in an Adner [[The Red Lisken|casino]] owned by Dunoche's associate [[Etsero]].<ref name="ND" />

Upon the [[Battle of the Great Pit of Carkoon|death]] of the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba]]<ref name="WbC" /> earlier in 4 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|ABY]],<ref name="NEC" /> Etsero found that his debt to the Hutt had been transferred to an unnamed party allied with the fragmented [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], with his [[Casino/Legends|casino]] being used to launder [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] for Imperial interests in the Yataga [[Sector/Legends|sector]]. In order to put an end to the fraud and suspected [[Death/Legends|murders]] being carried out in ''The Red Lisken'',<ref name="WbC" /> in [[7 ABY/Legends|7 ABY]]<ref name="Date">[[Wanted by Cracken (sourcebook)|''Wanted by Cracken'']] is dated to three [[Standard year/Legends|years]] after the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]]. Since ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates that conflict to [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], the placement of the sourcebook therefore translates to [[7 ABY/Legends|7 ABY]].</ref> the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] dispatched an [[Unidentified New Republic agent (The Red Liskin)|agent]] to the casino; however, the agent never reported in with his superiors and was suspected to have been dealt with by Etsero. New Republic [[General/Legends|General]] [[Airen Cracken/Legends|Airen Cracken]] subsequently described Etsero and his operations on Adner in ''[[Wanted by Cracken]]'', a [[Datafile/Legends|datafile]] that served as a compilation of reports on various threats to the New Republic.<ref name="WbC" />

==Inhabitants==
{{Main|Adnerem}}
[[File:Adnerem-AE.png|thumb|right|140px|The Adnerem were native to Adner.]]
Adner was the homeworld of the Adnerem, a species of tall, slender<ref name="Journal11" /> [[Mammal/Legends|mammalian]]<ref name="Alien Encounters">''[[Alien Encounters]]''</ref> [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoids]].<ref name="CSWE">{{CSWECite|I|12|Adnerem}}</ref> The natives of Adner had reached the [[Information Age]] level of technology. Imports on the planet were mainly specialty items and information products. Over [[Time/Legends|time]], the primitive tribal bands of the prehistorical Adnerem evolved into a complex social system based around the steris, a group of five to a hundred individuals unbound by [[Gene/Legends|genetic]] ties and dedicated to providing mutual support and collective advancement. This system was already well in place by the time Adner was discovered by off-worlders.<ref name="Journal11" />

Despite the steris orientation of their society, individual Adnerem were relatively asocial and introverted, and most members of the species were encountered on Adner as opposed to elsewhere in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. Overly large steri were traditionally frowned upon in the Adnerem society and were usually forcibly reduced in size; therefore, to increase their resource base, the steri managing Adner's entertainment industries chiefly hired members of other species to work there. As a result, a visitor in the entertainment zones of Adner was far more likely to encounter non-Adnerem than Adnerem.<ref name="Journal11" />

==Locations==
{{Quote|Not only is Etsero still paying, but his gambling house is now being used to launder credits for Imperial interests in that sector of the galaxy.|Airen Cracken, ''Wanted by Cracken''|<ref name="WbC" />}}
Adnerem lived in [[City/Legends|towns]], villages, and in more isolated [[Farm/Legends|farms]] and compounds. The buildings known as [[steris-house]]s were usually single- or two-floored earth-bermed disks. The buildings' round shape helped conserve and evenly distribute heat at night, while the earth provided insulation from heat during the day. Adner also featured casinos, pleasure houses, and [[Amusement park|theme parks]], which were visited by hundreds of thousands of tourists and thrill-seekers.<ref name="Journal11" /> The largest casino on Adner, ''The Red Lisken'', was run by the Adnerem Etsero<ref name="WbC" /> and his small but very affluent steris.<ref name="Journal11" />

==Behind the scenes==
Adner was first mentioned in the [[1993]] sourcebook [[Wanted by Cracken (sourcebook)|''Wanted by Cracken'']], which was written by [[Louis J. Prosperi]] for [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''.<ref name="WbC" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Adner [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore the planet Adner, in grid square N-16.<ref name="TEA Adner" />
